summary.intent: Address Greptile code review comments on PR #708 in the moltis repo, then request a re-review.
summary.outcome: All three review comments resolved: subscription filter drops events without sessionKey, flaky timeout removed, and 7 new 'requires sessionKey' tests added (34 total passing). Changes committed and pushed.

repo: GraphQL subscriptions are implemented in crates/graphql/src/subscriptions/mod.rs and must filter events lacking a sessionKey in the payload.
Integration tests live in crates/graphql/tests/integration.rs; the project uses cargo fmt and clippy as part of the pre-commit/CI workflow.

code.finding: Subscription handler must explicitly drop chat events that have no sessionKey in the payload to avoid leaking events across sessions.

An unnecessary timeout in the subscription test was causing flakiness; removing it stabilized the suite. All remaining subscription operations needed corresponding 'requires sessionKey' test coverage.

workflow: Greptile is used as an automated reviewer on PRs; re-review is requested after addressing comments.
Worktrees are used for parallel branch work under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/.
